reviews.review_description:
Gives Toy Trains VR a run for its money. The overall scene is very gothic and stark, too glaringly bright. A more ergonomic comfortable setting like a large warehouse mixed with a hookah lounge meets The Polar Express meets Victorian London. And have a Christmas option. Even if you make a DLC and charge money for it, I'd be willing to pay money to give that a shot!
Otherwise one of the very best experiences in VR imaginable.

One day later: I found the desert level. Teleport to the coral with the oasis and the tree. That leads to a Grand Canyon with water set up.
I would give this app 25 stars out of five. Better than a whole lot of other VR apps.
There is slight, mild, acute, chronic, and profound. Trainer VR would rate as profound.

You can set it to night with streetlights that turn on. This is one of the greatest VR apps in the history of VR. This app should win an Oscar for Best VR App of the Year.


Paraphrasing Pulp Fiction, 
This is Panda and this is Bava,. They're about $300 a gr**. And they're both really really good. But this is Choco from the Hartz Mountains of Germany and this one is a motherf*cking madman. It's $500 a gr** but when you do it, you will know where that extra $200 went. 


Trainer VR as it is, it's really really good. If you were to do a Christmas snowy world like the desert world you did but snowy with old style European Christmas building, multiclor lit trees and a Polar Express Baldwin S37 steam locomotive, that would be like Choco from the Hartz Mountains of Germany and who wants to be a Millionaire? The developer of hl this app would make a million at least if you released a Christmas DLC and we would be willing to pay. It would probably be really good.

I trying to figure out how to load and save, I accidentally wiped out all except one of the default demo models that were there originally when first installed. Is there any way to get those models on permanent display so they can be studied and learned from? 
The only way to get them back is to completely uninstall and discard. But would that mean having to purchase again? And then it would also wipe out the diorama I already spent about 25 hours on.

developer_response.body: Thank you! You can uninstall and reinstall the game at any time, there’s no need to purchase it again.

reviews.review_description:
Overall, good game but

 •relaxing most of the time. ￼

•Movement is annoying to say the least only four directions. 
￼
•The parts selection screen is too big and clunky.

• Wish there were more parts and just add pages (the selection screen)

ALSO PAGE TWO  AND THREE
of the 
SLIDESHOW  HOW  PLEASe

Great game for those who love trains runs smoothly but needs more. There’s only two trains you can choose so like to see more trains added in especially iconic trains from around the world

More themes I know you got a wild west theme but like to see city themes and countryside themes.

Road layout as well as trains you’ve got cars and vans you can select like to see road layouts to go with the railway.

Game has got potential if more updates come out.

reviews.review_title: Not bad at all but could be better
reviews.review_description:
Would love a toy train in vr that would be similar to laying the Lionel tracks as a kid- yes, I know that is exactly what is presented here but how about…. First of all, more tracks and track types. Not even sure- does this have train sounds? And does it offer a view from the engine as it runs?
Would love to see where this goes.  One thing I like is the enormous area to work with. 
Of course, would love to see more realistic Lionel type trains and track but that might be too much to ask….
How cool would it be to have that virtual Lionel layout without spending thousands or taking up a room in the house?
I would pay a lot for that
Dont get wrong its a great game, but what are these stupid UFOs taking whatever i build? Im just trying to make a big station and all my stuff is just took! Fix this.
Here’s a suggestion what about multiplayer? Imagine building train tracks with your friends that would be amazing!

reviews.review_title: Great game, wish it had a little "more"
reviews.review_description: I played this game on PS VR and loved it.  I am playing it on the Oculus Quest 2 and still love it.  I wish you could fit more elements on the screen at once.  As it is, I was building a pretty elaborate layout when the aliens started taking away my pieces.  If not possible to have more pieces, maybe adding some kind of "memory counter" near the top would help with planning.    Figuring out the logic for switch tracks and such is something I really enjoy.  It's cool sitting back and watching multiple trains run around the track, stopping at cranes or passenger stations based on how you set up the buttons.  My only other critique is that it would be great if you could somehow share creations.  My brother has the same game and it'd be cool if we could show off what we built without having to take picture or record video.  Other than that, please keep up the development.  No other train app is quite like this one.

reviews.review_title: Love to play this with my son
reviews.review_description: My son is massively into trains, and I’ve had this title off Steam for some time now. Building his favorite wooden tracks, and letting him tell me what he wants next. This game is amazingly fun, and super easy to pick up and play. However I would love to see hand tracking, so I could actually put the headset on my son, and let him try. Controls are still to big for him. 5 stars for a amazing sandbox game.

Very basic graphics and controls. Limited trains, scenery, buildings, cars, tracks, people, and such. For what this is, it is fun, but that fun is short-lived. Maybe this was a first step in developing a more robust game. Gotta start somewhere, right? There is potential for this to be an amazing train sim, which I, for one, would love to see.

reviews.review_title: Basically okay, but missing a lot
reviews.review_description: This is not a bad vr train game. The problem is a lot of the train tracks that come in real life for whatever reason are amiss. There are Y tracks but Z track connections are absent. - those are absolutely must items and using Y tracks to create Z connections is basically throwing off the entire program since it is extremely bad. Al ot of C tracks are way too sharp on the turn and require creating prolonged S patterns. Overall missing a lot of basic critical tracks but it is probably the best toy train Sim in vr out there

It didn't caught my daughter's attention so I refunded, but later on she remembered and wanted to try again. She likes it a lot! I didn't played with the game just helped out when there was some confusion. 

Overall the game looks polished, it is stable, on quest 1 if you have a lot on the screen it could get a bit laggy. The scenery being a white plain is a bit empty, some other environments could make it a lot better. 

There a lot of props to decorate with, tracks have variety. It's a missed out opportunity to not have roads for the cars IMO. 

User interace is easy to use but it cannot be hidden with a button, you have to close it with the UI "x".

It could use something else than sandbox mode.

Adding multiplayer would be awesome!

I hope it will get some updates!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review! You have the West scenario, you will see the entrance in the blank scenario, use teleport to go there!

reviews.review_title: Fun to construct and then ride the train
reviews.review_description:
This is a great app. It's fun to construct a train set then drive through it in first person. 

But it looks like a work in progress. Hopefully the developer will finish and polish it up.  A few requests if you don't mind for future updates. 

1) Instructions on driving the train would help. What does the lever on the boiler cover do? 

2) I can't seem to adjust the speed well enough to keep the train on the tracks. An "Auto-pilot" option would be nice. 

3) Can you remap the hand gripping action to the grip button when driving the train? It's counterintuitive since other apps use this button to grip stuff.

4) A 45 curve track is a must. 

5) Point and place track placement would definitely help. I got sick moving around so much to place the tracks. Plus I got a sore neck from it. See “Trains VR” for the Go/GearVR as an example. I don’t want to waste all my time moving and placing tracks. 

Thanks to the developers. I am looking forward to really getting into this app.

A must buy if you have children. My son absolutely loves playing this. I felt some nostalgia playing it myself lol.
The action button is a really cool feature.
Hopefully there will be more pieces for the action button to come.
This should really be in the official store.

I've spent a lot more time in this app than I have in many which cost a lot more!  Feels a little bit basic but as it isn't in the proper app store yet, I will be forgiving!  lol! Hopefully the developers will add to the game, some additional scenery and ability to change colours on the trains would be nice as would a longer duration on the cloud that appears over trains when they collide - I frequently hear the explosion but can't turn around fast enough to see where the collision happened!  Very frustrating when you have a big layout!  :)   Still, very enjoyable as it is!

Grandson loves this game.  Unlike when I played train sets Took ages to build up and more importantly ..does not fill the room and floor and is is easy to clear away..brilliant you can save and store sites you have built…only down side is the ground it is just white ..no grass or any thing…roadways would be great too

Nice game for the price, I'd be willing to pay more for more content.
Great concept !

reviews.review_title: Good games but I got some suggestions
reviews.review_description:
All right now I’m not gonna draw this out but the game is perfect not perfect but you know it’s beautiful and somewhere no here’s the things that I would add or like to be added most likely I would want a little more riding the trains no I’m not saying the trains suck I’m just saying I me a little more train varieties like one with a coal hopper or tender for all you British people a lot of other varieties of Hogwarts train caboose all those. Another map because I mean the desert map after a while The map just kind of gets pretty boring after a while anyway there is a much probably what I would want know what I would want like a foresee map or in the winter map those would be cool
What besides all that were made the game kudos to you￼ i’m pretty sure that I use it sorry I I feel like I’m drunk it’s like what 8:25 PM I am basically drunk at this point anyways this is a good game but just a little more trains or something you know it’ll kill him kill niceness you know I’m saying I’m gonna stab auto correct it’s jacking up my entire thing might her speech Open anyways good game bye.

I love this game.As a model railway guy , this was a great experience;but i was disappointed by the lack of diversity with the trains. Try adding a mallard/flying scotsman/Tornado /class66 or 37 as I would love that. Also could you please add a countryside map or city map .
